title: rubus × malignus
intro: Rubus × malignus, a hybrid of the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus), is a rare and lesser-known species of raspberry native to North America. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its anthocyanins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus × malignus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various health conditions. Rubus × malignus can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and jams, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
